key_points: Dorm quality varies; freshmen assigned seemingly randomly
New student center was under construction for years
Students can try to 'finesse' better dorms via housing application
key_quotes: this year they said there was no question thingy to finesse a good dorm
Went here 2015-19 and the new student center was under construction!

key_points: Greek life dominates: 74% participation rate is unusually high
Non-Greek students risk social isolation after rush season
Campus feels emptier than expected given marketed enrollment size
key_quotes: after rush takes place...freshman that don't rush get kind of cut off from everyone who does join greek life
Its almost marketed as a larger school than what it is. I been to one dining hall...where are the students
6-7 hours of hw a day?? That's crazy!!!

key_points: One dining hall visited had many choices but nothing exceptional
Food quality described as adequate but unremarkable
Limited student comments suggest dining is not a standout feature
key_quotes: I been to one dining hall and it did have a lot of choices. But nothing amazing
The campus is nice. But when he said Pharmacy he probably made a good choice

key_points: STEM students report extreme stress culture, not from difficulty but peer pressure
6-7 hours of homework daily reported by students
Pre-med track intensity creates high anxiety environment
key_quotes: stem majors at Emory are hard because of the stress people evoke rather than the material or people being cutthroat
Not taking chem this semester despite having other STEM classes in my courseload was such a blessing in disguise
6-7 hours of hw a day?? That's crazy!!!

key_points: Financial aid packages can be generous but require active fighting to maintain
Budget cuts have hit financial aid hard per current students
International students face particular uncertainty around aid continuity
key_quotes: you would need to accept the fact that you have to fight for your aid. They're experiencing some budget cuts and its hitting hardest in the financial aid
I might have problems with it? should I still consider Emory?
They offered $80k, I appealed and got an extra $10k

key_points: Goizueta Business School grades on a curve, creating competitive atmosphere
CS program consistently flagged as not highly ranked
Pre-med and health sciences are genuine strengths with strong research opportunities
key_quotes: I had no idea that the business school was graded on a curve
I heard that Emory's CS program isn't too highly ranked
I would vouch for Emory in your position, especially since you're pre-med

key_points: Financial aid office requires students to appeal and advocate for themselves
Budget cuts affecting student resources are acknowledged by current students
Emory deliberately times decision releases for Wednesday 6pm so students are home with family
key_quotes: you would need to accept the fact that you have to fight for your aid
Emory always does this. They announce when the decision will come out and that decision comes out on a Wednesday at 6pm so you can be home with your family
They're experiencing some budget cuts and its hitting hardest in the financial aid

key_points: Beautiful campus but students risk staying in the 'Emory bubble' all 4 years
Atlanta access requires local connections or a car; campus feels isolated
Non-Georgians advised to befriend locals to access Atlanta's real social scene
key_quotes: I did see people stay in the Emory bubble all 4 years and thought why?
If y'all aren't from Georgia, make friends with Georgians...people from GSU, Georgia Tech, and the AUC have a lot more access
Atlanta technically has a more urban culture than the other big cities despite being sprawly

key_points: Strong networking in Atlanta especially for healthcare and public health careers
CS recruiting from top companies is limited given program's lower ranking
Alumni feel stronger ties to professional/grad school than Emory due to weak alumni network
key_quotes: the networking you can get at a school like that in a big city is excellent
the biggest issue with Emory was a lack of school spirit, community, and alumni networking. After I left Emory, I actually felt closer ties to my professional school
I heard that Emory's CS program isn't too highly ranked...do good companies recruit there?

key_points: Full or near-full rides available and heavily praised by recipients
Without strong aid, cost-to-prestige ratio questioned versus peers
Students with aid packages overwhelmingly advised to attend; sticker price students should negotiate hard
key_quotes: Go to Emory unless your dad can absolutely prevent it
Choosing URI over a full ride at Emory would be insanity
you would need to accept the fact that you have to fight for your aid

key_points: Pre-professional, high-achieving atmosphere; less a traditional college experience
Low school spirit and weak campus community identity noted by alumni and students
Academically serious but socially fragmented, especially if not in Greek life
key_quotes: the biggest issue with Emory was a lack of school spirit, community, and alumni networking
I always like were are the students. They are there but its not as many as you would think
when he said always wanted to change the school spirit LMAO

key_points: **Financial Aid:** Budget cuts actively reducing packages; requires appeals to maintain aid
**Greek Life:** 74% participation creates two-tier social system that excludes non-rushers
**CS Program:** Consistently flagged as weak; poor recruiting from top tech companies
key_quotes: you would need to accept the fact that you have to fight for your aid. They're experiencing some budget cuts
freshman that don't rush get kind of cut off from everyone who does join greek life
I heard that Emory's CS program isn't too highly ranked...do good companies recruit there?

key_points: Woodruff and other merit scholarships can fully cover attendance for top students
Oxford College two-year entry offers tight-knit community before main campus
Atlanta's AUC and Georgia Tech connections expand social and academic options significantly
key_quotes: If y'all aren't from Georgia, make friends with Georgians...GSU, Georgia Tech, and the AUC have a lot more access
I was a woodruff scholar many years ago and have stayed part of the scholars network
Small, tight-knit community, so easier to make friends and build connections with professors
